payload.title: Operator-surface message sending needs PC-level serialization
payload.summary: If more than one operator surface attempts to send an operator-surface message at the same time, overlapping focus/clipboard/type/submit windows can interfere with each other. The current bridge has a timing-sensitive delivery phase, including stabilization waits, so concurrent senders should not run independently.
payload.hazard: Two or more send attempts can race during focus changes, clipboard replacement, input escrow, typed delivery, or submit delay; this may deliver text to the wrong surface, submit partial input, fail to restore focus, or corrupt operator attention state.
proposal: Introduce a central PC-level operator-surface send intent queue owned by the PC runtime locus.
Treat each send as an intent that is admitted, serialized, executed, and evidenced by the PC-level queue worker.
Reject or queue overlapping sends rather than allowing concurrent Send-OperatorSurfaceInput.ps1 executions.
Use a lock/lease with timeout and stale-lock recovery for the active input bridge critical section.
Expose queue status and current active send in operator-surface health/doctor output.
Keep Site-level agents as requesters; the PC Site remains authority over focus/clipboard/input effects.
payload.control_boundary: The serialization point belongs at PC runtime locus because the shared resources are PC-local: foreground window, keyboard/mouse hooks, clipboard, HWND bindings, and submit timing.
acceptance_criteria: Concurrent send requests cannot execute focus/clipboard/type/submit phases simultaneously.
Second send either waits in queue, receives busy/deferred status, or fails closed with repair guidance.
Evidence records ordering, requester, target identity, start/end time, and whether it was queued or rejected.
Interrupted/stale active sends can be recovered without leaving the queue permanently blocked.
payload.source_context: Observed while using operator-surface messaging to coordinate CPY/Narada agents; overlapping sends during the bridge delay window would be unsafe.

task.title: Serialize operator-surface message sending at PC locus
